### Todos

#### 15. CodeLens Summary ‚Äî Click-to-Show Rich HTML Panel

> *Moved from Phase 2 (#12) ‚Äî rolled back during Phase 3 debugging*

**Problem:** Currently the CodeLens summary item has:
- **Hover:** Shows plain-text tooltip via `Command.tooltip` (string-only, no formatting)
- **Click:** No-op (`showCommentTooltip` command does nothing)

The user wants the opposite: **no tooltip on hover**, **rich HTML content shown on click**.

**Root cause:** Phase 1's spike confirmed `Command.tooltip` only accepts strings. This led to a plain-text tooltip as the "best effort" ‚Äî but the intended UX is click-triggered rich content, not hover.

**Approach:** Use `vscode.window.createWebviewPanel()` to show a themed HTML panel when clicking the CodeLens summary. The extension already has webview infrastructure (`anchorsGridProvider.ts`, `anchorsGridWebview.ts`) so this pattern is established.

**Changes:**
1. **Remove tooltip from CodeLens summary** ‚Äî delete the `tooltip` property from the summary CodeLens item in `commentCodeLensProvider.ts`
2. **Implement `showCommentTooltip` command** ‚Äî replace the no-op with logic that:
   - Receives `(uri, startLine)` arguments
   - Finds the comment block at that location
   - Renders it as styled HTML (theme-aware colors for method names, params, types, etc.)
   - Opens a `WebviewPanel` (or reuses an existing one) with the rendered content
3. **Create `renderToHtml()` function** ‚Äî new function in `commentRenderer.ts` that produces styled HTML with:
   - Theme-aware colors (method name, parameter, type, comment text colors)
   - Proper section layout (Summary, Parameters table, Returns, Remarks, Example with code styling, Exceptions)
   - CSS that respects VS Code theme variables (`--vscode-editor-foreground`, etc.)
4. **Panel behavior:**
   - Reuse a single panel (don't open a new one per click)
   - Panel title: method/member name or "XML Documentation"
   - Panel closes on Escape or when clicking a different CodeLens updates the content in-place

**Files:** `src/rendering/commentCodeLensProvider.ts`, `src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts`, `src/extension.ts`

#### 16. Native CodeLens Tooltip ‚Äî Investigate Suppression

**Problem:** When hovering over the CodeLens summary text, VS Code shows a native tooltip displaying the command title. This is VS Code's built-in behavior for all CodeLens items ‚Äî it always shows the `Command.title` string as a tooltip.

**Root cause:** VS Code's CodeLens rendering always shows a hover tooltip. There is no official API to suppress it. Setting `Command.tooltip` to `''` (empty string) may suppress the tooltip text, though VS Code may still show the command title as fallback.

**Investigation:**
- Test setting `tooltip: ''` on the summary CodeLens `Command` ‚Äî this is the most likely way to suppress
- If that doesn't work, test `tooltip: '\u200B'` (zero-width space) as a workaround
- Document findings either way

**Files:** `src/rendering/commentCodeLensProvider.ts`

---

#### 17. Flickering / Crash When Typing in Comments

**Problem:** Typing inside an XML doc comment (e.g., starting `NOTE:`) causes the entire comment block to flicker rapidly. Status bar icons ("analyzing files", "test discover") also flicker. The extension eventually crashes and rendering mode switches to OFF.

**Root cause:** The `onDidChangeTextDocument` handler in `extension.ts` (line 256) calls THREE decoration systems on EVERY keystroke with NO debouncing:

```
decorationManager?.onDocumentChanged(editor, event);  // ‚úÖ Has internal 1500ms suppression
anchorDecorationManager?.updateDecorations(editor);    // ‚ùå NO debounce ‚Äî full document rescan
prefixHighlighter?.updateDecorations(editor);          // ‚ùå NO debounce ‚Äî full document rescan
```

`anchorDecorationManager.updateDecorations()` scans every line of the document, checks for anchor tags, rebuilds all decoration ranges, and re-applies them. On every single keystroke. Same for `prefixHighlighter`. This causes a cascade of rapid decoration disposal + re-creation that causes visible flickering and can overwhelm VS Code's rendering pipeline.

Additionally, the `debouncedAnchorRescan` (line 264) triggers workspace-level anchor scanning on each edit, adding more load.

**Fix:** Add debouncing to the `onDidChangeTextDocument` handler for `anchorDecorationManager` and `prefixHighlighter`:
1. Create debounce timers for anchor decorations and prefix highlighting (same pattern as `decorationManager.editTimers`)
2. On text change: clear existing timers, set new timers (e.g., 500ms delay)
3. Only call `updateDecorations()` when the timer fires
4. This matches how `decorationManager` already handles it (1500ms `EDIT_SUPPRESSION_DELAY`)

**Files:** `src/extension.ts`

---

#### 18. `</para><para>` Text Concatenation Bug ‚Äî "NIf"

**Problem:** When XML content contains `</para><para>` (adjacent para elements), text from consecutive paragraphs is concatenated without spacing in the CodeLens summary. Example: paragraph ending "...N" followed by paragraph starting "If..." renders as "NIf" in the summary.

**Root cause:** `extractPlainText()` in `commentRenderer.ts` (line 1103) joins all child text parts with `parts.join('')` ‚Äî empty string separator. When processing a `<summary>` containing multiple `<para>` elements, the text from each paragraph is concatenated directly:
- Para 1 text: `"...something N"`
- Para 2 text: `"If condition..."`  
- Result: `"...something NIf condition..."`

The full-content rendering (`renderCommentBlock`) handles this correctly by inserting blank lines around `<para>` tags (lines 596-599), but the summary extraction (`getStrippedSummaryFromXml` ‚Üí `extractPlainText`) bypasses that rendering path entirely and does raw text extraction.

**Fix:** Change `extractPlainText()` to add a space when recursing into block-level elements like `<para>`:
- For `<para>` children: push `' '` before the child's text
- This ensures word boundaries are preserved in the plain-text summary
- Alternative: change `parts.join('')` to `parts.join(' ')` but this would over-space inline elements like `<c>`, `<see>`, `<paramref>`
- Better approach: handle `<para>` specifically in the switch statement, prepending a space

**Files:** `src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts`

---

#### 19. Documentation Overlay on Code Anchors Pane

**Problem:** Clicking the CodeLens summary opens a full `WebviewPanel` in a new editor column. The user wants a lightweight popup that appears within the existing Code Anchors pane and dismisses on click-away.

**Approach:** Overlay on the existing Code Anchors `WebviewView`:
1. Click CodeLens summary ‚Üí extension focuses Code Anchors pane via command, then sends `postMessage` with rendered doc HTML to the anchors grid webview
2. The webview JavaScript shows a floating overlay card on top of the grid, sized dynamically to pane height with scrollbar if content exceeds available space
3. Dismiss triggers: clicking outside overlay, clicking ‚úï button, pressing Escape
4. On dismiss: overlay hides, focus returns to code editor via `postMessage` ‚Üí extension calls `showTextDocument`
5. Clicking a different CodeLens summary updates overlay content in-place

**Changes:**
- **`src/anchors/anchorsGridWebview.ts`**: Add overlay container HTML, CSS (position: absolute, z-index, dynamic height, overflow-y: auto, backdrop click handler), and JS (show/hide/escape/dismiss logic, postMessage for focus-return)
- **`src/anchors/anchorsGridProvider.ts`**: Add `showDocOverlay(html: string)` method that posts a `showDocOverlay` message to the webview. Add message handler for `dismissOverlay` that returns focus to editor.
- **`src/extension.ts`**: Replace `WebviewPanel` logic in `showCommentTooltip` command with: focus Code Anchors pane, call `anchorsGridProvider.showDocOverlay(html)`. Remove `docPanel` variable entirely.
- **`src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts`**: `renderToHtml()` should return just the body content (no `<!DOCTYPE>`, `<html>`, `<head>`) since it'll be injected into the existing webview's overlay div. CSS styles can be embedded inline or in a `<style>` block within the content.

**Files:** `src/anchors/anchorsGridWebview.ts`, `src/anchors/anchorsGridProvider.ts`, `src/extension.ts`, `src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts`

---

#### 20. Documentation Overlay ‚Äî Formatting Per User Spec

**Problem:** The rendered HTML doesn't match the user's desired formatting. Based on screenshots and the original spec from Phase 1 (#8), the desired format is:

```
MethodName: Summary text here

    param1 ‚Äî Description of first parameter
    param2 ‚Äî Description of second parameter

Returns: Description of return value

Remarks:
    Remarks content here

Example:
    Example code here
```

**Key formatting requirements:**
- Method name prominently displayed with summary inline
- Parameters indented with `‚Äî` separator
- Section labels (Returns, Remarks, Example) as simple headers, not uppercase/bordered
- Compact and readable ‚Äî no heavy visual chrome
- Theme-aware colors for different semantic elements (method name, params, types)

**Fix:** Rewrite `renderToHtml()` CSS and layout to match the spec:
- Simplify section labels ‚Äî plain bold text, no uppercase/borders/letter-spacing
- Keep parameter layout (already close to spec with `name ‚Äî description`)
- Simplify example styling (indented code, not heavy blockquote)
- Remove excessive margins and visual weight
- Content will render inside the overlay div on the anchors grid pane

**Files:** `src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts`

---

#### 21. Anchor Tag Colorization in Rendered Comments

**Problem:** Anchor tags (TODO, HACK, NOTE, BUG, etc.) appearing within XML doc comment text are not colorized in either the CodeLens summary or the HTML documentation panel. The live editor colorizes them via `AnchorDecorationManager`, but the rendered output treats them as plain text.

**Root cause:** `processMarkdownInText()` in `markdownProcessor.ts` handles markdown formatting (bold, italic, code, links) but does not detect anchor tag patterns. The `segmentToHtml()` function has no anchor-aware segment type. The `AnchorDecorationManager` only works on live editor decorations, not on rendered output.

**Fix:** Add anchor tag detection to the rendering pipeline:
1. Add `SegmentType.Anchor` to the `SegmentType` enum in `types.ts`
2. Add `anchorColor?: string` to `RenderedSegment` interface
3. In `processMarkdownInText()` (or a post-processing step), detect anchor patterns (TODO:, HACK:, NOTE:, BUG:, FIXME:, UNDONE:, REVIEW:, ANCHOR:) in text segments and split them into `Anchor` segments with their configured color
4. In `segmentToHtml()`, render `Anchor` segments with inline color styling using the anchor's configured color from `BUILTIN_ANCHOR_TYPES`
5. In `segmentToMarkdown()`, render with bold/color if applicable

**Files:** `src/types.ts`, `src/rendering/markdownProcessor.ts`, `src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts`

### Phase 3: Summary of Changes ‚úì

#### #16 ‚Äî Tooltip Suppression
Set `tooltip: ''` (empty string) on the summary CodeLens `Command` object in `commentCodeLensProvider.ts`. This suppresses the VS Code native hover tooltip that was showing the command title text. VS Code still renders the CodeLens text itself, but the hover popup is eliminated.

#### #17 ‚Äî Flickering Fix (Critical)
**Root cause:** In `extension.ts`, the `onDidChangeTextDocument` handler was calling `anchorDecorationManager.updateDecorations()` and `prefixHighlighter.updateDecorations()` directly on every keystroke with NO debounce. Each call performed a full document rescan, causing visible flickering and performance degradation that could crash the extension to OFF state.

**Fix:** Added 500ms debounce timers (`anchorDecUpdateTimer` and `prefixDecUpdateTimer`) in `extension.ts`. Both decoration update calls are now wrapped in `setTimeout` with `DECORATION_UPDATE_DELAY = 500` ms, using the same re-capture pattern for the active editor. The `decorationManager.onDocumentChanged()` call remains direct since it has its own internal 1500ms edit suppression.

#### #18 ‚Äî Para Concatenation Fix
**Root cause:** `extractPlainText()` in `commentRenderer.ts` had no handling for `<para>` elements ‚Äî they fell through to the `default` case which recursively extracted text but with no separator. When `<summary>` contained `</para><para>` patterns, text from adjacent paragraphs concatenated directly (e.g., "N" + "If" = "NIf").

**Fix:** Added explicit `case 'para':` in the `extractPlainText()` switch statement that prepends a space (`' '`) before recursively extracting the child text. This ensures word boundaries between paragraph elements.

#### #19 ‚Äî Documentation Overlay on Anchors Pane
**Replaced:** The `WebviewPanel` approach (which opened a new editor tab) with a floating overlay card on the existing Code Anchors grid webview.

**Changes:**
- Added overlay HTML/CSS/JS to `anchorsGridWebview.ts`:
  - Semi-transparent backdrop (`rgba(0,0,0,0.3)`) that dismisses on click
  - Centered overlay card with max 600px width, dynamic height with scrollbar
  - Close button (‚úï) in top-right corner
  - Keyboard dismiss on Escape
  - All VS Code theme variables for consistent theming
- Added `showDocOverlay(html)` method to `AnchorsGridProvider` class
- Added `overlayDismissed` message handler that returns focus to active editor
- Rewired `showCommentTooltip` command in `extension.ts`:
  - Focuses the anchors grid pane via `kat-comment-studio.anchorsGrid.focus`
  - Sends HTML fragment via `showDocOverlay()` instead of creating a WebviewPanel
  - Uses new `renderToHtmlFragment()` (body content only, no HTML wrapper)
- Removed `docPanel` module variable and `renderToHtml` import from `extension.ts`

#### #20 ‚Äî Overlay Formatting
Added new `renderToHtmlFragment()` export to `commentRenderer.ts` that returns just the HTML body content without the `<!DOCTYPE>` wrapper. This is used by the overlay which has its own CSS defined in the anchors grid webview. The full-document `renderToHtml()` was refactored to call `renderToHtmlFragment()` internally, eliminating code duplication. Overlay CSS in `anchorsGridWebview.ts` includes themed styles for all section types (summary, params, returns, remarks, example, exceptions, see-also).

#### #21 ‚Äî Anchor Tag Colorization in Rendered HTML
**Added:** Automatic colorization of anchor tags (TODO:, HACK:, NOTE:, BUG:, FIXME:, UNDONE:, REVIEW:, ANCHOR:) in rendered HTML output.

**Implementation:**
- Imported `BUILTIN_ANCHOR_TYPES` into `commentRenderer.ts`
- Built a static color lookup (`ANCHOR_TAG_COLORS`) from the anchor service definitions
- Added `colorizeAnchorTags()` helper function that wraps matched tags with `<span style="color:...;font-weight:600">`
- Applied in `segmentToHtml()` for `SegmentType.Text` segments ‚Äî all text content now has anchor tags colorized using the same colors as inline editor decorations

### Files Modified (Phase 3)

- `src/extension.ts` ‚Äî Added debounce timers for anchor/prefix decoration updates; rewired showCommentTooltip to use overlay; removed docPanel and renderToHtml import
- `src/rendering/commentCodeLensProvider.ts` ‚Äî Added `tooltip: ''` to suppress native CodeLens hover
- `src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts` ‚Äî Fixed `<para>` handling in `extractPlainText()`; added `renderToHtmlFragment()` export; added anchor tag colorization in `segmentToHtml()`; imported `BUILTIN_ANCHOR_TYPES`
- `src/anchors/anchorsGridWebview.ts` ‚Äî Added overlay HTML structure, CSS styles, and JS dismiss logic (backdrop click, close button, Escape key)
- `src/anchors/anchorsGridProvider.ts` ‚Äî Added `showDocOverlay()` method and `overlayDismissed` message handler

### Test Results

- All 157 tests passing
- TypeScript compilation clean
- No new dependencies added

## Phase 4: Rich Hover Tooltip ‚Äî Research & Analysis

### Background

The current approach shows XML doc comment content via a webview overlay in the Code Anchors panel. The user identified that VS Code's own built-in XML doc hover (from the C# language server) and extensions like GitLens produce rich, formatted hover tooltips directly in the editor. Investigation to determine if we can do the same.

### Research Findings

#### How VS Code's Built-in C# Hover Works

The hover tooltip with colorized type signatures (e.g., `DateTime DateTimeExtensions.FirstOfMonthOrCoincident(this DateTime dt)`) is provided by the **C# language server** (OmniSharp / C# Dev Kit). It returns a `MarkdownString` containing a fenced code block:

```markdown
```csharp
(extension) DateTime DateTimeExtensions.FirstOfMonthOrCoincident(this DateTime dt)
`` `
```

VS Code's markdown renderer then applies **syntax tokenization** to the code block using the C# TextMate grammar, producing the colored keywords, types, and parameter names seen in the screenshot. This is NOT custom HTML/CSS ‚Äî it's the same syntax highlighting used in the editor.

The "Returns:" section and description text below the signature are plain markdown text rendered normally.

#### How GitLens Does Rich Hovers

GitLens uses `vscode.MarkdownString` with:
- `isTrusted = true` ‚Äî enables `[text](command:gitlens.action)` command links (the clickable buttons: copy SHA, open diff, browse repo, etc.)
- `supportThemeIcons = true` ‚Äî enables `$(git-commit)`, `$(copy)`, etc. for inline icons
- Standard markdown formatting ‚Äî bold, italic, code spans, horizontal rules
- `supportHtml = true` ‚Äî for structural HTML (`<div>`, `<span>`, `<table>`, etc.)

The "buttons" are actually **command links**: `[$(icon) Label](command:extensionId.commandName "tooltip")`. They look like buttons but are just styled markdown links that execute VS Code commands.

#### What `MarkdownString.supportHtml` Actually Allows

VS Code sanitizes HTML in MarkdownStrings through DOMPurify with a whitelist:

**Allowed tags** (75 total): `a`, `abbr`, `b`, `bdo`, `blockquote`, `br`, `caption`, `cite`, `code`, `col`, `colgroup`, `dd`, `del`, `details`, `dfn`, `div`, `dl`, `dt`, `em`, `figcaption`, `figure`, `h1`‚Äì`h6`, `hr`, `i`, `img`, `ins`, `kbd`, `label`, `li`, `mark`, `ol`, `p`, `pre`, `q`, `rp`, `rt`, `ruby`, `s`, `samp`, `small`, `source`, `span`, `strike`, `strong`, `sub`, `summary`, `sup`, `table`, `tbody`, `td`, `tfoot`, `th`, `thead`, `time`, `tr`, `tt`, `u`, `ul`, `var`, `video`, `wbr`

**Allowed attributes**: `href`, `target`, `src`, `alt`, `title`, `for`, `name`, `role`, `tabindex`, `x-dispatch`, `required`, `checked`, `placeholder`, `type`, `start`, `width`, `height`, `align`

**‚öÝÔ∏è NOT allowed**: `style`, `class`, `data-*` ‚Äî these are stripped during sanitization.

#### Implications for Our Extension

**What we CAN do in a hover:**
- ‚úÖ Bold section headings (`**Returns:**`)
- ‚úÖ Inline code for parameter names (`` `paramName` ``)
- ‚úÖ Syntax-highlighted code blocks (` ```csharp ... ``` `) ‚Äî arguably BETTER than the overlay since VS Code applies real tokenization
- ‚úÖ Tables for parameter lists (`<table>`, `<tr>`, `<td>`)
- ‚úÖ Horizontal rules (`---`) between sections
- ‚úÖ Theme icons (`$(symbol-parameter)`, `$(symbol-method)`)
- ‚úÖ Command links for interactive elements (`[text](command:...)`)
- ‚úÖ Structured HTML layout (`<div>`, `<span>`, `<p>`, `<details>`)

**What we CANNOT do in a hover:**
- ‚ùå Custom colors (no `style` attribute ‚Äî anchor tag colorization impossible)
- ‚ùå CSS classes (no `class` attribute ‚Äî can't apply custom styling)
- ‚ùå Custom backgrounds on sections
- ‚ùå Custom font sizes or spacing
- ‚ùå The exact visual fidelity of the webview overlay

#### Existing Infrastructure

The extension ALREADY has the hover provider implemented but not registered:

- **`src/rendering/commentHoverProvider.ts`** ‚Äî `CommentHoverProvider` class implements `vscode.HoverProvider`, uses `renderToMarkdown()`, has `setEnabled(boolean)` toggle. Currently dead code ‚Äî never registered in `extension.ts`.
- **`src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts`** line 143 ‚Äî `renderToMarkdown()` already produces `MarkdownString` with `supportHtml = true`, `isTrusted = true`, structured section rendering, all section types handled.
- **`src/extension.ts`** line 89 ‚Äî `LinkAnchorHoverProvider` is registered but `CommentHoverProvider` is not.

To enable the hover, we literally just need to register it:
```typescript
const commentHoverProvider = new CommentHoverProvider();
vscode.languages.registerHoverProvider(SUPPORTED_LANGUAGES, commentHoverProvider);
```

#### Suppressing the Built-in C# Hover

VS Code **merges all hover providers' results** vertically ‚Äî there is no API to suppress another extension's hover contributions. Options:

1. **Accept stacking**: Our hover appears alongside the C# hover. Ours shows the full doc comment content; theirs shows the type signature. This is arguably complementary, not redundant.
2. **Language-scoped settings**: We could add `configurationDefaults` in `package.json` to disable `editor.hover.enabled` for C# ‚Äî but this disables ALL hovers for C# including ours. Not viable.
3. **C# Dev Kit settings**: The C# extension may have a `dotnet.inlayHints` or similar setting, but there's no documented setting to disable only the doc comment portion of its hover.
4. **Conditional registration**: We can register/unregister our hover provider based on `renderingMode`. When rendering is "on" and comments are folded, the hover provides the folded content. When "off", we skip registration so the C# hover works normally.

**Recommendation**: Option 1 (accept stacking) + Option 4 (conditional registration). When rendering mode is "on", our hover shows the rich doc content. The C# hover's type signature info is complementary and not harmful. When rendering mode is "off", no duplicate content.

### Updated Research: `<span style>` IS Allowed

The initial Phase 4 analysis stated `style` and `class` attributes are NOT allowed. **This was only partially correct.** Deeper investigation of VS Code's DOMPurify configuration (in `src/vs/base/browser/markdownRenderer.ts`) reveals:

- **`style` IS allowed on `<span>` elements** ‚Äî restricted to:
  - `color` with hex (`#XXXXXX`) or VS Code CSS variables (`var(--vscode-editor-foreground)`)
  - `background-color` with hex or CSS variables
  - `border-radius` with pixel values
  - Regex: `/^(color\:(#[0-9a-fA-F]+|var\(--vscode(-[a-zA-Z0-9]+)+\));)?(background-color\:(#[0-9a-fA-F]+|var\(--vscode(-[a-zA-Z0-9]+)+\));)?(border-radius:[0-9]+px;)?$/`
- **`class` IS allowed on `<span>` elements** ‚Äî restricted to `codicon codicon-[a-z\-]+`

This changes the feasibility assessment substantially. We CAN achieve colored text, colored parameter names, anchor tag colorization (TODO:, HACK:, etc.), and theme-aware colors in hover popups.

### How GitLens Renders Profile Icons

GitLens uses Markdown image syntax in `MarkdownString`:
```
![title](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/{userId}?size={size}|width=X,height=X "title")
```

Avatar URLs: GitHub (`https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/{userId}?size=N`) or Gravatar fallback (`https://www.gravatar.com/avatar/{hash}?s=N`). Also uses `data:image/svg+xml;base64,{svg}` for SVG presence indicators. The `<img>` tag is in the allowed DOMPurify whitelist with `src`, `width`, `height` attributes.

### Recommendation (Updated)

Two display modes controlled by `popupStyle` setting:

1. **Hover** (default, `popupStyle: 'hover'`) ‚Äî Rich `MarkdownString` popup triggered by CodeLens click. Uses:
   - `<span style="color:var(--vscode-*);">` for theme-aware colored section labels and parameter names
   - `<span style="color:#HEX;">` for anchor tag colorization (TODO:, HACK:, etc.)
   - Fenced code blocks (` ```csharp `) for syntax-highlighted examples
   - Markdown tables for parameter lists
   - Theme icons `$(symbol-parameter)` etc. for section markers
   - `---` horizontal rules for section dividers

2. **Overlay** (current, `popupStyle: 'overlay'`) ‚Äî Full HTML rendered in anchors grid webview. Full CSS control, custom backgrounds, close button, etc. Available as alternative for users who prefer the richer visual fidelity.

### Todos

#### 26. Register CommentHoverProvider ‚úÖ (Implemented)

Wire up existing `CommentHoverProvider` in `extension.ts`. Modified to use a "pending block" one-shot pattern: `setPendingHover(uri, startLine)` arms it, `provideHover()` returns rich content once then clears. This ensures hover only fires on CodeLens click, not on regular mouse hover.

**Changes:**
- `src/rendering/commentHoverProvider.ts` ‚Äî Added `_pendingUri`/`_pendingStartLine` fields, `setPendingHover()`, `clearPending()`, match on `startLine` not `position`
- `src/extension.ts` ‚Äî Import and register `CommentHoverProvider`, connect to `renderingMode` config changes

#### 27. Rewrite renderToMarkdown() for Rich Visual Output ‚úÖ (Implemented)

Complete rewrite of `renderToMarkdown()` to produce visually rich MarkdownString using allowed HTML subset:

- **Summary**: Plain text with anchor tag colorization
- **Parameters**: Grouped into a single styled table with colored parameter names using `<span style="color:var(--vscode-symbolIcon-fieldForeground);">`
- **Returns/Value/Remarks**: Muted section headings with theme icons
- **Example**: Code-only lines merged into fenced ` ```csharp ` blocks for real syntax highlighting
- **Exceptions**: Warning icon + inline code for exception type name
- **Section dividers**: `---` horizontal rules
- **Anchor tags**: Colorized with `<span style="color:#HEX;">` using existing `ANCHOR_TAG_COLORS` map

New functions: `sectionHeadingMarkdown()`, `renderParamTableMarkdown()`, `sectionLinesToMarkdownRich()`, `sectionLinesToMarkdownRichExample()`, `segmentToMarkdownRich()`, `colorizeAnchorTagsMarkdown()`

**Files:** `src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts`

#### 28. Add popupStyle Configuration ‚úÖ (Implemented)

New setting `kat-comment-studio.popupStyle` with values `'hover'` (default) | `'overlay'`.

- `showCommentTooltip` command reads `popupStyle` at invocation time
- `'hover'`: arms `CommentHoverProvider`, positions cursor at `startLine`, executes `editor.action.showHover`
- `'overlay'`: existing behavior (renders HTML fragment, focuses anchors panel, shows overlay)

**Files:** `src/types.ts`, `src/configuration.ts`, `package.json`, `src/extension.ts`

### Files Modified (Phase 4)

- `src/types.ts` ‚Äî Added `PopupStyle` type, `popupStyle` to `CommentStudioConfig`
- `src/configuration.ts` ‚Äî Added `popupStyle` config read with `PopupStyle` import
- `package.json` ‚Äî Added `kat-comment-studio.popupStyle` setting definition
- `src/rendering/commentHoverProvider.ts` ‚Äî Rewritten with pending-block one-shot pattern
- `src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts` ‚Äî New `renderToMarkdown()` with rich formatting, new helper functions, `colorizeAnchorTagsMarkdown()`
- `src/extension.ts` ‚Äî Register `CommentHoverProvider`, import, config change wiring, `showCommentTooltip` dual-mode logic

**Wire up the existing `CommentHoverProvider`** as the primary tooltip for doc comments. Keep the overlay as a secondary "full view" via CodeLens click. This gives:

1. **Hover** ‚Äî quick inline preview when mousing over a folded comment. Appears instantly without focus shift. Uses VS Code's native code block syntax highlighting for examples.
2. **Overlay** ‚Äî full-featured view with custom styling (colors, backgrounds, anchor colorization) accessible via clicking the CodeLens summary. Keeps the visual richness.

### Todos

#### 26. Register CommentHoverProvider

**Problem:** `CommentHoverProvider` exists in `src/rendering/commentHoverProvider.ts` but is never registered.

**Fix:**
- In `extension.ts`, import and instantiate `CommentHoverProvider`
- Register via `vscode.languages.registerHoverProvider(SUPPORTED_LANGUAGES, commentHoverProvider)`
- Wire `commentHoverProvider.setEnabled(renderingMode === 'on')` into the configuration change handler
- Consider: only show hover when the block is folded (if expanded, user can read the original XML). Check `decorationManager.isFolded(docKey, startLine)` or similar.

**Files:** `src/extension.ts`

#### 27. Improve renderToMarkdown() Output Quality

**Problem:** The existing `renderToMarkdown()` works but may need enhancement for hover-quality output. Needs evaluation of:
- Are example sections rendered as syntax-highlighted code blocks (` ```csharp `) or plain code?
- Are parameter lists formatted as clean tables or just text?
- Does it handle all the same section types as `renderToHtmlFragment()`?

**Fix:** Audit `renderToMarkdown()` and `segmentToMarkdown()` against the HTML renderer. Improve formatting for hover presentation:
- Example `<code>` blocks ‚Üí ` ```csharp ... ``` ` fenced code blocks
- Parameter lists ‚Üí consider `<table>` for alignment (if `supportHtml` is on)
- Section labels ‚Üí `**bold**` headings with theme icons like `$(symbol-parameter)` Parameters

#### 28. Conditional Hover Based on Fold State

**Problem:** When rendering mode is "on" and a comment is expanded (user explicitly clicked Expand), showing the hover is redundant ‚Äî the user can already read the XML. Hover should only appear when the comment is folded.

**Fix:**
- In `CommentHoverProvider.provideHover()`, check if the block is currently folded before returning hover content
- This requires the hover provider to have access to `DecorationManager`'s expanded blocks state
- Options: pass a callback/getter to the hover provider, or expose `isFolded(docKey, startLine)` on DecorationManager

## Phase 3b: Post-Implementation Bug Fixes ‚Äî Current

Bugs discovered during manual testing of the Phase 3 overlay and fold-state features.

### Todos

#### 22. Overlay Doesn't Close When Clicking Back in Editor

**Problem:** After opening the doc overlay via CodeLens click, clicking back into the editor leaves the overlay open. The overlay only closes via its own explicit dismiss actions (backdrop click, ‚úï button, Escape key). There's no mechanism to detect that focus has returned to the editor.

**Root Cause:** The anchors grid webview has no way to know when the user clicks away from it. VS Code webviews don't fire focus-loss events that bubble to extension code. The only hooks are in the extension host ‚Äî `onDidChangeTextEditorSelection` and `onDidChangeActiveTextEditor`.

**Fix:**
- Add a `hideOverlay()` method to `AnchorsGridProvider` that posts a `{ type: 'hideDocOverlay' }` message to the webview
- In `anchorsGridWebview.ts`, add a `hideDocOverlay` case in the message handler that calls `hideDocOverlay()` (same as dismiss, but does NOT post `overlayDismissed` back to avoid circular messaging)
- In `extension.ts`, listen to `vscode.window.onDidChangeTextEditorSelection` ‚Äî when any selection changes in any editor, call `anchorsGridProvider?.hideOverlay()`
- Guard: only send if overlay is actually open (track `_overlayVisible` boolean in provider, set true in `showDocOverlay()`, set false in `overlayDismissed` handler and `hideOverlay()`)

**Files:** `src/anchors/anchorsGridProvider.ts`, `src/anchors/anchorsGridWebview.ts`, `src/extension.ts`

---

#### 23. Overlay Width ‚Äî 80% of Anchor Pane

**Problem:** The overlay card is `max-width: 600px; width: 90%`. User wants it to be 80% of the anchor pane width.

**Root Cause:** CSS values are just wrong.

**Fix:**
- In `anchorsGridWebview.ts`, change `.doc-overlay-card` CSS:
  - `width: 90%` ‚Üí `width: 80%`
  - Remove `max-width: 600px` (let it scale naturally with the pane)

**Files:** `src/anchors/anchorsGridWebview.ts`

---

#### 24. Example Section CSS ‚Äî Background Only on `<code>` Blocks

**Problem:** The `<example>` section has TWO visible backgrounds stacked: the `.example .section-body` wrapper has a `textBlockQuote-background` fill + left border, and then `<code>` elements inside it have their own smaller background. The user wants a single background ‚Äî only on `<code>` content, spanning full width like the current example section does.

**Root Cause:** The CSS applies a blockquote-style background to the entire `.example .section-body`, and then `<code>` has its own inline background on top. These layer visually.

**Fix:**
- In `anchorsGridWebview.ts`, strip the background/border/padding/radius from `.doc-overlay-card .example .section-body`:
  - Remove `background`, `border-left`, `border-radius`
  - Keep `padding-left: 16px` for indentation (same as other section bodies)
  - Remove `font-family`, `font-size`, `white-space` (those belong on code, not the wrapper)
- Restyle `.doc-overlay-card .example code` (or add new rule `.doc-overlay-card .example .section-body code`) to be block-level with full-width background:
  - `display: block`
  - `background: var(--vscode-textBlockQuote-background, #1e1e1e)`
  - `border-left: 3px solid var(--vscode-textBlockQuote-border, #444)`
  - `padding: 8px 12px`
  - `border-radius: 3px`
  - `white-space: pre-wrap`
  - `margin: 4px 0`

**Files:** `src/anchors/anchorsGridWebview.ts`

---

#### 25a. Collapse CodeLens Reopens Immediately (Cursor Inside XML)

**Problem:** When the cursor is inside an expanded XML comment and the user clicks "Collapse Xml" CodeLens, the block folds but immediately auto-expands again. The collapse "doesn't stick."

**Root Cause:** In `decorationManager.ts`, `toggleFold()` (line 478) folds the block but **restores the cursor to its saved position** inside the comment (line 494: `editor.selections = savedSelections`). After folding, the `onSelectionChanged` handler fires, detects the cursor is inside a folded block (line 245-256), and starts the 500ms `startExpandTimer` which unfolds it right back.

**Fix:**
- In `toggleFold()`, when **folding** (the `expanded.has(startLine)` branch), instead of restoring `savedSelections`, move the cursor to `block.endLine + 1` (the first line after the comment block ‚Äî typically the declaration line).
- This requires passing the full block info (or at least `endLine`) into `toggleFold()`. Currently it only receives `startLine`. Either:
  - Change signature to accept the block object or `endLine`, OR
  - Look up the block from `getCachedCommentBlocks` inside `toggleFold()` using `startLine`
- After folding, set `editor.selections` to a cursor at `(endLine + 1, 0)` instead of `savedSelections`
- This moves the cursor out of the comment, so `onSelectionChanged` won't trigger auto-expand

**Files:** `src/rendering/decorationManager.ts`

---

#### 25b. Scroll-Away Causes Transparency and "Expand Xml" State Bug

**Problem:** When a comment block is expanded and the cursor is inside it, scrolling up via mouse wheel so the cursor line leaves the viewport causes: (a) the entire comment becomes transparent (dim decoration applied), and (b) the CodeLens changes from "Collapse Xml" to "Expand Xml" ‚Äî as if the block auto-folded.

**Root Cause:** In `decorationManager.ts`, `syncFoldStateFromVisibleRanges()` (line 293) checks whether the block's interior lines are visible. It specifically checks only `startLine + 1` (line 312-313: `vr.start.line <= interiorStart && vr.end.line >= interiorStart`). When the user scrolls the block off-screen, the first interior line is no longer in any visible range, so the code concludes "gutter fold detected" (line 323-329) and collapses the block ‚Äî even though the block was actually just scrolled away, not gutter-folded.

The check conflates two scenarios:
1. **Gutter fold:** Block's start line IS visible, but interior lines are NOT (folded via the editor chevron)
2. **Scrolled off-screen:** Neither start line NOR interior lines are visible (just scrolled out of view)

**Fix:**
- In `syncFoldStateFromVisibleRanges()`, add a guard: only treat a block as gutter-folded if the block's **start line IS visible** but interior is NOT. If the start line itself is not in any visible range, the block is just scrolled off-screen ‚Äî don't change its fold state.
- Add a check before the `!interiorVisible && wasExpanded` branch (line 323):
  ```typescript
  const startLineVisible = visibleRanges.some(vr =>
    vr.start.line <= block.startLine && vr.end.line >= block.startLine
  );
  ```
- Change condition at line 323 from:
  ```typescript
  } else if (!interiorVisible && wasExpanded) {
  ```
  to:
  ```typescript
  } else if (!interiorVisible && wasExpanded && startLineVisible) {
  ```
- This means: "only fold if we can see the start line but NOT the interior ‚Äî i.e., it was genuinely gutter-folded." If neither is visible, ignore it.

**Files:** `src/rendering/decorationManager.ts`

---

## Phase 4b: Post-Hover Testing Polish ‚úÖ (Implemented ‚Äî commit `6592f05`)

Bugs found during initial manual testing of the Phase 4 hover popup.

### Todos

#### 29. Suppress Auto-Expand on Hover Trigger ‚úÖ

**Problem:** Clicking the CodeLens summary to trigger the hover popup moves the cursor to `startLine`, which fires `onSelectionChanged` ‚Üí `startExpandTimer`, causing the folded XML comment to auto-expand.

**Fix:** Added one-shot `_suppressAutoExpand` flag on `DecorationManager`. `suppressNextAutoExpand()` called from `showCommentTooltip` before moving cursor. Flag cleared on next `onSelectionChanged` regardless of outcome.

**Files:** `src/rendering/decorationManager.ts`, `src/extension.ts`

#### 30. Code Block Wrapping ‚Üí `<pre>+<span>` ‚úÖ (Superseded by #32)

**Problem:** Fenced markdown code blocks (` ```csharp `) wrap in VS Code hovers due to `white-space: pre-wrap`.

**Fix:** Switched to `<pre><span style="color:...;background-color:...;border-radius:3px;">` for no-wrap and custom background. Traded syntax highlighting for scroll control.

**Note:** User prefers syntax highlighting ‚Äî see #32 which reverts this back to fenced blocks.

**Files:** `src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts`

---

#### 31. CodeLens Summary Truncation ‚úÖ

**Problem:** Long summary text in CodeLens caused layout overflow when combined with VS Code's References CodeLens on narrow viewports.

**Fix:** Truncated summary text at 100 characters with `‚Ä¶` ellipsis. Hardcoded ‚Äî see #33 for making this configurable.

**Files:** `src/rendering/commentCodeLensProvider.ts`

## Phase 4c: Hover Polish Round 2 ‚Äî Pending

Refinements based on user feedback after testing Phase 4b.

### Todos

#### 32. Revert Code Blocks to Fenced Markdown

**Problem:** Phase 4b (#30) switched code blocks from fenced markdown to `<pre>+<span>` HTML. The `<span>` element is **inline**, so `background-color` only extends to the text width of each individual line ‚Äî creating a striped/ragged look where shorter lines have narrower backgrounds than longer lines. This is a fundamental limitation: VS Code's DOMPurify only allows `style` on `<span>` (inline), not on block elements like `<pre>`, `div`, or `code`.

User prefers syntax highlighting over no-wrap, and fenced code blocks solve both problems:
1. **Syntax highlighting** ‚Äî VS Code tokenizes fenced ` ```csharp ` blocks
2. **Contiguous background** ‚Äî VS Code renders fenced blocks as block-level elements with a uniform background that extends full-width, eliminating the stripe effect

The only trade-off: the background color is controlled by VS Code's theme (typically a slightly darker shade), not by us. We cannot customize it. But the visual result is far better than the striped `<span>` approach.

**Fix:**
- In `sectionLinesToMarkdownRich()` in `commentRenderer.ts`, revert the code block rendering from:
  ```typescript
  `\n<pre><span style="color:...;background-color:...;">${codeTexts.join('\n')}</span></pre>\n`
  ```
  back to:
  ```typescript
  `\n\`\`\`csharp\n${codeTexts.join('\n')}\n\`\`\`\n`
  ```
- Remove the `escapeHtml()` call on code segments (fenced blocks handle escaping natively)
- Restore JSDoc comment text

**Files:** `src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts`

---

#### 33. Make CodeLens Summary Max Length Configurable

**Problem:** Phase 4b (#31) hardcoded `MAX_CODELENS_LENGTH = 100` inside `commentCodeLensProvider.ts`. The VS Code Extension API has **no way to dynamically detect visible editor columns** ‚Äî `TextEditor` exposes `visibleRanges` (line ranges) but no viewport width/column count. The number of visible columns depends on window size, sidebar state, font size, zoom level, minimap, line numbers, glyph margin, etc.

Since dynamic detection is impossible, the truncation length should be a user-configurable setting so users on different screen sizes can tune it.

**Fix:**
1. **`package.json`** ‚Äî Add new setting:
   ```json
   "kat-comment-studio.codeLensMaxLength": {
     "type": "number",
     "default": 100,
     "minimum": 30,
     "maximum": 500,
     "description": "Maximum number of characters for the CodeLens summary text before truncation"
   }
   ```
2. **`src/types.ts`** ‚Äî Add `codeLensMaxLength: number` to `CommentStudioConfig`
3. **`src/configuration.ts`** ‚Äî Read the new setting
4. **`src/rendering/commentCodeLensProvider.ts`** ‚Äî Replace hardcoded `100` with `config.codeLensMaxLength`. The provider already receives config via `updateConfiguration()`.

**Files:** `package.json`, `src/types.ts`, `src/configuration.ts`, `src/rendering/commentCodeLensProvider.ts`

---

### Files Modified (Phase 4c ‚Äî planned)

- `src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts` ‚Äî Revert code blocks to fenced markdown (#32)
- `package.json` ‚Äî New `codeLensMaxLength` setting (#33)
- `src/types.ts` ‚Äî Add `codeLensMaxLength` to config interface (#33)
- `src/configuration.ts` ‚Äî Read `codeLensMaxLength` (#33)
- `src/rendering/commentCodeLensProvider.ts` ‚Äî Use configurable max length (#33)

## Phase 4d: Code Block Background Investigation ‚Äî Pending

### Analysis: Why Code Blocks Have No Custom Background

The current fenced markdown approach (` ```csharp `) relies on VS Code's built-in code block styling, which applies a subtle theme-controlled background. This may be nearly invisible in some themes. The previous `<pre><span>` approach created visible **stripes** due to a CSS limitation.

**Root cause of stripes:** CSS inline elements (`<span>`) render `background-color` per-line with gaps equal to `line-height - font-size`. When a `<span>` wraps multi-line text inside `<pre>`, each line gets its own background strip separated by line-height gaps. This is fundamental CSS behavior (`box-decoration-break: slice` is the default), and VS Code's DOMPurify doesn't allow overriding it.

**DOMPurify constraints (from VS Code source `src/vs/base/browser/markdownRenderer.ts`):**
- `style` is allowed ONLY on `<span>` elements
- Only `color`, `background-color`, and `border-radius` properties are permitted
- No `display`, `padding`, `line-height`, `box-decoration-break`, or any layout property
- `style` is stripped from `<pre>`, `div`, `code`, `table`, `td`, and all other elements

**Why wrapping fenced blocks in `<span>` might work differently:** When VS Code's markdown renderer encounters a fenced block, it converts it to `<pre><code class="language-csharp">...</code></pre>`. This is a **block-level** element. If we wrap the entire fenced block in `<span style="background-color:...">`, the browser might render the block-level `<pre>` as a child of the inline `<span>`, and the background could extend behind the entire block. This differs from the `<pre><span>text</span></pre>` approach where the span was INSIDE the pre ‚Äî here the span would be OUTSIDE it.

### Todos

#### 34. Attempt Fenced Code Block with `<span>` Background Wrapper

**Approach:** Wrap the fenced markdown code block in a `<span>` with `background-color:var(--vscode-editor-background)`:

```typescript
parts.push(
  `\n<span style="background-color:var(--vscode-editor-background);">\n\n${fence}csharp\n${codeBlock}\n${fence}\n\n</span>\n`
);
```

**Why this MIGHT work (different from previous attempt):**
- Previous: `<pre><span style="bg">code</span></pre>` ‚Äî span was INSIDE pre, inline background fragmented per line
- New: `<span style="bg">` wrapping the MARKDOWN fence ‚Äî VS Code's renderer converts the fence to `<pre><code>` (block-level) inside the span. Browsers often propagate inline parent backgrounds behind block children.

**Why this might NOT work:**
- VS Code's markdown‚ÜíHTML pipeline may close the `<span>` before the block element
- DOMPurify may restructure the DOM, separating the span from the code block
- If it doesn't work, fall back to plain fenced blocks (VS Code's default code block background)

**Fallback if span wrapper fails:** Accept VS Code's theme-provided code block background. Document as a known limitation.

**Files:** `src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts`
